title: Parkside survey - Daniel Francis MP | Bexleyheath and Crayford
text_excerpt: Parkside survey - Daniel Francis MP | Bexleyheath and Crayford Find me on Facebook 0207 219 5278 HOME ABOUT NEWS CAMPAIGNS NEWSLETTERS USEFUL LINKS Useful Links Advice & Support Legislative Update: Key Government Bills HELP Contact Visit Parliament Event Request Home » Parkside survey Parkside survey 01/05/2026 Posted by: Daniel Francis MP Category: News No Comments Share on Social Media Parkside Community Centre: What Local Residents Told Me Last year, Parkside Community Centre in Barnehurst was demolished by the London Borough of Bexley, leaving families, community groups and local residents without a vital shared space. Parkside was more than just a building. It hosted a nursery, youth groups, music lessons, fitness classes, community events and served as a polling station. For many families – including my own – it was part of everyday life. Since its demolition, the site has remained empty and unused. Bexley Council has said it wants the site to remain in community use – but only if a charitable organisation can fund and build a new centre themselves. This approach puts all the financial risk on local groups, many of whom are already stretched. It creates a real danger that no organisation will be able to take this on, and that Parkside is lost forever. If the council is genuine about keeping this site for the community, it must do more. I Asked Local Residents for Their Views To make sure residents’ voices were heard, my office launched a survey of people who live locally or previously used Parkside Community Centre. Local residents responded, and the message was clear: of respondents said their household had previously used Parkside Community Centre said the centre was important or very important to their household said the council itself should rebuild the community centre said they would oppose the site being sold to developers 70% were unaware that the council had no plans to rebuild the centre itself Residents highlighted the loss of: childcare and family activities spaces for young people opportunities for older residents local hub within walking distance Barnehurst now has no community centre, with the nearest alternatives over 20 minutes away on foot or by public transport. Survey respondents were clear about the future they want for Parkside: like‑for‑like community centre, rebuilt on the existing site for families, children and young people to support health, wellbeing and reducing loneliness welcoming, inclusive hub that brings neighbours together Above all, residents want the council to take responsibility and ensure that Parkside remains a community asset. What Happens Next I have shared these findings publicly and raised the issue in Parliament to highlight the wider impact of losing community spaces like Parkside. I will continue pressing Bexley Council on its current approach to rebuilding Parkside Community Centre to ensure this site for community use in the long term Powered By EmbedPress Share on Social Media KEEP UPDATED SUBSCRIBE FOR EMAIL UPDATES Keep Updated Subscribe to my email newsletter to keep updated on our news, campaigns and work in the local area.
